purpose: Root React component that owns view routing, IPC bootstrap, modal hosting, and the floating Claude chat panel. Calls useDashboardData() once at the top level to establish all IPC push listeners (initialization/logs/agent_progress/all_progress/init_state/queue_changed/tasks_unblocked/write_rejected). Mounts a dual shell: app-shell-chat (ChatSidebar + chat surface) and app-shell-code (Sidebar + code surface) — both always mounted, hidden via CSS based on state.appMode so chat IPC listeners never unmount. Renders DashboardContent with WavePipeline/ChainPipeline keyed off task.type, the action bar, ProgressBar, ReplanningBanner (circuit breaker), TimelinePanel, BottomPanel and modal dialogs (commands, project, planning, settings, logs).
gotchas: ClaudeFloatingPanel is always mounted (kept in tree even when activeView !== 'claude') so IPC listeners inside ClaudeView do not unmount during background swarm runs — toggling visibility uses style.display, not conditional rendering.
Both chat-shell and code-shell are mounted simultaneously (hidden via CSS class 'hidden') — relying on conditional renders here would break chat IPC continuity when switching appMode.

ClaudeFloatingPanel manually wires its own left-edge drag-to-resize via mousemove/mouseup listeners on document AND also calls useResize(floatRef, viewMode) — there are TWO separate resize systems coexisting (legacy mouse-events + new pointer-events).
Width style is cleared (floatRef.current.style.width = '') when leaving 'expanded' mode via prevMode ref tracking — without this, inline drag widths bleed into minimized/collapsed/maximized states.
fetchInitialData uses api.invoke?.('api', { path: '/api/...' }) form, NOT the dedicated IPC methods — odd because main.jsx fetch shim has dedicated routes; this is fallback path.
handleArchiveClick uses fetch('/api/archives/...') which goes through main.jsx fetch shim and routes to api.getArchive() via IPC — not a real network request despite the URL.
Header is rendered ONCE at top — both chat and code shells share the same Header component.
patterns: View-router uses switch on activeView in renderMainContent / renderChatContent — do NOT add new views via conditional && chains, follow the switch pattern.
Modal dispatch uses { type: 'OPEN_MODAL', modal, dashboardId } and { type: 'CLOSE_MODAL' } — modal name (not boolean) drives which modal renders; activeModal is single-string-or-null in state.
ProgressSection extracts active_task from currentStatus; it computes display values inline rather than receiving props because mergeState in useDashboardData already populates totals.
ClearDashboardSection archives BEFORE clearing — archiveDashboard then clearDashboard chained via .catch(() => {}) — failures swallowed silently.
Project name shown in action bar is derived by stripping trailing slashes and pop()-ing last path segment — same algorithm used in ClaudeFloatingHeader.
Replanning banner detection lives in mergeState (useDashboardData), not here — App just renders if task.overall_status === 'replanning'.
conventions: JSX file extension (.jsx) with default export named App.
Section comments use box-drawing characters: // ── SectionName ────
Inline SVG icons (no icon library) with viewBox 16x16 and stroke-width 1.3-1.5.
useState for purely-local UI flags (timelineOpen, selectedAgent, showConfirm); global state goes through dispatch.
